name: Strawberry Snow
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, let egg whites stand at room temperature for 1 hour.
2. Meanwhile, drain strawberries and reserve the syrup. Sprinkle gelatin over syrup in a small saucepan; stir until gelatin is dissolved.
3. Turn strawberries, 1/4 cup sugar, lemon juice, salt, a few drops of food coloring, 1/4 cup water, and the gelatin into a blender container. Cover; blend on low speed for 1 minute.
4. Set mixture in pan of ice and water. Let stand, stirring occasionally, just until beginning to set - about 20 minutes.
5. With an electric mixer at high speed, beat egg whites until soft peaks form when the beater is slowly raised. Gradually beat in remaining sugar. Continue beating until stiff peaks form.
6. With the same beater, at high speed, beat gelatin mixture until light. Fold in beaten egg white until well blended. Turn into 1-quart mold.
7. Refrigerate until firm - at least 3 hours.
8. To serve: Run a small spatula around edge of mold. Invert over serving plate; place a hot, damp dishcloth on bottom of mold; shake gently to release. Serve with Custard Sauce.
Custard Sauce:
1. In the top of a double boiler, over direct heat, heat milk until tiny bubbles appear around the edge of the pan.
2. Beat egg yolks and sugar together to mix well.
3. Very slowly pour hot milk into egg mixture, beating constantly.
4. Return mixture to top of double boiler; place over hot, not boiling, water. The water in the lower part of the double boiler should not touch the upper part.
5. Cook, stirring constantly, until a thin coating forms on metal spoons - 8 to 10 minutes.
6. Pour custard immediately into a bowl; place sheet of waxed paper directly on surface.
7. Set bowl in cold water to cool. Stir in vanilla. Refrigerate.
